explanation: This structured natural language property translates the requirement regarding mode transitions between PCV and PSV. The natural language specifies that the transition from PCV to PSV shall only occur if 'ventilation is on' at the end of a PCV inspiratory time and if the user has issued the mode change command (from the GUI). The chosen template 'only while bool_exp1, _ABSTRACT_VAR1_' captures the restriction that these transitions are only allowed when 'ventilating' (ventilation is on). The second template 'upon bool_exp2, _ABSTRACT_VAR2_' specifies that when the required conditions (the end of inspiratory phase and receipt of command to change to PSV mode) are met, the transition logic should be applied. The final template 'immediately satisfy bool_exp3' expresses that, under the above triggering conditions, the system should switch to PSV mode immediately. The constructed boolean expressions reflect these specifics: 'ventilating' ensures ventilation is on; 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected' encodes that change can happen only at the end of inspiration and upon receiving the change command; 'PSVMode' asserts that the system must transition to PSV mode in these circumstances. This structure ensures full alignment with the conditions in the original requirement.
decision2_substring: the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI
decision3_substring: the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur
output_structured_natural_language: only while ventilating, upon inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected, immediately satisfy PSVMode

explanation: This structured property expresses the requirement that 'the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on,' and further qualifies that this transition should happen 'at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI.' 'Ventilation is on' is represented by 'ventilating'. The transition from PCV to PSV (i.e., PSVMode should become active) is gated upon ending a PCV inspiration, which is indicated by 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd', but the transition must follow a command from the GUI ('PSVModeSelected'). The property thus states: Whenever ventilation is on, upon both inspiratory phase ending AND the PCV-to-PSV change-command being given, PSVMode must be immediately satisfied (i.e., active next).
decision2_substring: at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI
decision3_substring: the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur
output_structured_natural_language: whenever ventilating, upon inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected, immediately satisfy PSVMode

explanation: The original requirement specifies that when the system is in PCV mode with ventilation on, the mode should switch to PSV if a mode change is commanded by the user (PSVModeSelected) and only at the end of the PCV inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd). The structured property captures this by requiring that whenever the conditions of being in PCV mode AND ventilation AND PSV mode selection occur, then at the next occurrence of inspiratoryPhaseEnd, the system must immediately switch to PSV mode (i.e., PSVMode becomes active). This ensures the transition occurs only under the defined conditions and only at the end of the PCV inspiratory interval.

decision3_substring: the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur

explanation: This requirement specifies a mode transition: 'The transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on, and the transition shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command to PSV has been received from the GUI.' The atomic propositions relevant here are 'ventilating' (ventilation is on), 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' (end of PCV inspiratory time), and 'PSVModeSelected' (change mode command to PSV from GUI). The translation uses the pattern: 'while ventilating, whenever (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected), immediately satisfy PSVMode' — capturing that whenever ventilation is ongoing, at the end of the inspiratory phase and the GUI has selected PSV mode, the transition to PSV must immediately occur.

explanation: The requirement describes a transition condition from PCV to PSV modes in a ventilator. The transition should occur if (1) ventilation is on (ventilating), (2) the change mode command to PSV has been received (PSVModeSelected), and (3) it must occur specifically at the end of the PCV inspiratory time (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) if the change mode command has been received. From the atomic propositions, 'ventilating' captures ventilation being on, 'PSVModeSelected' captures the received command to switch to PSV, and 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' determines the end of the inspiratory phase. The translation segments the complex condition into two triggers. Only while PCVMode (current context is PCV), whenever (ventilating & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd), the mode must switch to PSV (represented as PSVMode).
decision2_substring: the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on, the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI.
decision3_substring: the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur
output_structured_natural_language: only while PCVMode, whenever ventilating & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd, immediately satisfy PSVMode

This is best captured by starting with 'while ventilating, whenever inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PCVMode & PSVModeSelected, transition to PSVMode' (here, the property is that PSVMode should become true). All identified atomic propositions are mapped accordingly.
